gad7.name: GAD-7 Anxiety Screening
gad7.description: Validated 7-question screening tool for generalized anxiety — score 0–21 with severity band.

meta.title: GAD-7 Anxiety Test (Generalized Anxiety) — Online Screening | Health Calculators
meta.description: Take the validated GAD-7 anxiety screening online. 7 questions, score 0–21, severity bands minimal/mild/moderate/severe. Free, anonymous, instant. Screening tool — not a diagnosis.

gad7.title: GAD-7 Anxiety Screening
gad7.description: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems? Pick exactly one answer per question. Your total score (0–21) and severity band will appear automatically.
gad7.introNote: Reference period: the past 2 weeks. The GAD-7 is a screening tool — not a medical diagnosis.

feelingNervous.title: 1. Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge

cantStopWorrying.title: 2. Not being able to stop or control worrying

worryingTooMuch.title: 3. Worrying too much about different things

restless.title: 5. Being so restless that it is hard to sit still

afraidSomethingAwful.title: 7. Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

hints.Minimal: Score 0–4 suggests minimal anxiety symptoms. No specific action is usually needed — keep monitoring how you feel.
hints.Mild: Score 5–9 suggests mild anxiety symptoms. Self-care, stress management and watchful waiting are appropriate. Re-test in 2–4 weeks.
hints.Moderate: Score 10–14 suggests moderate anxiety symptoms. Please consult a healthcare professional — further evaluation and a possible treatment plan are recommended.
hints.Severe: Score 15–21 suggests severe anxiety symptoms. Please consult a healthcare professional promptly. Active treatment is typically recommended.

gad7.incompleteHint: Answer all 7 questions to see your GAD-7 score.

scaleRows.label: Mild
scaleRows.hint: Symptoms mild — self-care, re-test in 2–4 weeks

gad7.howItWorksText: The GAD-7 was developed and validated by Spitzer, Kroenke, Williams and Löwe (Arch Intern Med, 2006). Seven items, each scored 0 (Not at all) to 3 (Nearly every day), sum to 0–21. A cutoff of 10 has the best balance of sensitivity (89 %) and specificity (82 %) for generalized anxiety disorder and is widely used as a referral threshold.
gad7.needsEvaluationLabel: Your score suggests further evaluation may be helpful
gad7.disclaimer: This is a screening tool, not a medical diagnosis. If your score is 10 or above, or you have concerns, please consult a healthcare professional.

faq.a: The GAD-7 screens for generalized anxiety disorder by asking how often you have been bothered by seven core symptoms over the last 2 weeks. It is widely used in primary care and mental-health settings.

faq.q: What do the score bands mean?
faq.a: 0–4 minimal, 5–9 mild, 10–14 moderate, 15–21 severe anxiety symptoms. A score of 10 or more is the commonly used cutoff that warrants further clinical evaluation.
No. The GAD-7 is a screening and severity tool — not a diagnosis. A clinician confirms or rules out an anxiety disorder using a full evaluation, your history and clinical judgement.

faq.q: How often should I take the GAD-7?
faq.a: It works well as a baseline and then every 2–4 weeks during treatment or when symptoms change. Trends across multiple tests are more meaningful than a single value.

faq.q: Can the GAD-7 also detect panic disorder, social anxiety or PTSD?
faq.a: It performs best for generalized anxiety, but has moderate accuracy for panic disorder, social anxiety disorder and PTSD. A specialist will use additional tools (PHQ-9, PCL-5, etc.) when those are suspected.

faq.q: What if I have thoughts of self-harm?
faq.a: The GAD-7 does not screen for suicidal thoughts. If you have thoughts of hurting yourself or others, contact emergency services or a crisis line immediately (in the US: 988; in Germany: 0800 1110111).

faq.q: Is the GAD-7 suitable for teenagers?
faq.a: The GAD-7 is validated for adults and is also used in adolescents from about age 13. For younger children, age-specific tools are preferred.
